assumptions_to_verify: Receipt-reported pytest runs were executed against this exact working tree state
Future reviewer outputs put evidence refs in critical_review.evidence_refs and tests in the top-level tests field as expected
Treating external refs as skipped is acceptable for replay rather than requiring network-backed verification
contradictions_checked: Majority vote risk: refuted by majority_vote_used=false and no weighting/threshold-based panel count logic in adjudicate_reviewer_panel
Block weakening risk: refuted by evaluate_reviewer_panel revise/deny handling and stdio cursor_decision=revise whenever panel decision is not accept
Vacuous evidence check risk: refuted by cwd confinement, sha256 compare, missing/external/unbounded classification, and bounded-refs test assertions
Artifact omission risk: refuted by state allowlist, read_gate_transcript projection, artifact markdown rendering, and export test coverage
Planning hash drift risk: refuted by shasum matching PRD/grill/issues/TDD/implementation plan hashes
missing_evidence: No independent pytest rerun in this read-only review; relying on provided/exported receipts
No live proof that a future reviewer-provided test command is executed, only that tests are captured and bounded as refs
Actual exported run did not contain an independent_reviewer_adjudication event because the reviewed panel did not split; behavior is covered by tests and code inspection
critical_review.strongest_objection: The implementation is not a separate semantic adjudicator; it deterministically chooses the strongest objection and checks bounded refs/hashes. If the intended acceptance required a new model/tool to re-judge factual correctness or execute cited tests, this should not advance. The planning artifacts explicitly narrowed the slice to deterministic packet assembly plus bounded evidence checks, so this is not blocking.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing focused/full-suite test run, a trace showing adjudication downgrading revise/deny to accept, or a requirement clarification that this slice must perform semantic re-adjudication or execute cited test commands rather than record bounded evidence checks.

outcome.confidence_rationale: Evidence receipts confirm that the full test suite passed, contradicting Claude's claim that tests were not run. The implementation meets all acceptance criteria.

claims: All 4 acceptance clauses backed by realized source + non-vacuous tests
Both NON-GOALs (no majority vote, no block weakening) structurally enforced
Minority revise/deny recovery already handled by conservative aggregator; adjudication additively adds all-accept+strong-objection->escalate path
confidence_criteria: artifacts export wired for adjudication event
5 named tests exist and assert non-vacuous statuses
outcome.confidence_rationale: Every acceptance clause and both NON-GOAL guards verified directly in the working tree with file:line evidence and non-vacuous tests; diff stat matches all prior gate memories. Capped below 0.95 solely because the explicit 'full suite green' clause was not empirically run (pytest approval declined), leaving deterministic-replay verified only by construction.

assumptions_to_verify: On-disk planning artifacts still match handoff sha256 (read directly; hashes not re-derived)
Conservative aggregator (prior slice) still blocks any minority revise/deny so adjudication's additive escalate path does not regress earlier coverage
contradictions_checked: Guard could weaken a block — refuted: stdio:1331-1339 only upgrades accept->escalate and cursor_decision:1340-1344 stays revise whenever panel!=accept
Adjudication uses majority vote — refuted: majority_vote_used False:488 and 'deliberately not a weighting function':425
Evidence check is vacuous stub — refuted: :596-642 resolves cwd-confined, sha256-compares, classifies external/unbounded/missing; test:3568 asserts all branches
missing_evidence: re-derived sha256 of implementation-plan.md manifest entry 9adcb043 (Bash hashing not performed)
critical_review.strongest_objection: 'Full suite green / deterministic replay' is an explicit acceptance criterion and pytest was never executed on this task; the accept verdict is inspection-based, not empirically test-verified.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A pytest run showing any of the 5 named tests or 4 regressions failing, or finding a code path where adjudication downgrades a real revise/deny to accept.

objections: Acceptance clause 'deterministic replay / full suite green' is empirically unverified because pytest was not run; verdict rests on code inspection plus deterministic-by-construction reasoning.

specialists.name: lead-direct-verifier
specialists.objection: full-suite-green clause empirically unverified (pytest not run)

outcome.summary: Final outcome_review: all 4 acceptance clauses realized in working tree (669 ins/6 files). Tool-backed adjudication (_check_evidence_refs sha256/cwd-confined), block-preservation guard never sets decision=accept, both NON-GOAL guards verified, 5 named tests non-vacuous. Sole residual: pytest not run (operator-declined).

claims: All 4 acceptance clauses backed by realized source + non-vacuous tests
Both NON-GOALs (no majority vote, no block weakening) structurally enforced
Minority revise/deny recovery already handled by conservative aggregator; adjudication additively adds all-accept+strong-objection->escalate path
